task: SPEC-IROH-BLAKE3-FEDERATION-TRANSPORT: direct peer-to-peer federation lane using Iroh for node identity/QUIC, iroh-blobs BLAKE3 transfer links over unchanged sha256 identity, SubstratePeer delta freshness, iroh-gossip awareness, per-peer trust policy, and an opt-in driver/doctor surface.

items.deliverable: Verify first: pin compatible iroh, iroh-blobs, and iroh-gossip crate versions in a standalone app crate, prove they build independently, and bind implementation choices to current docs/crate metadata.
items.acceptance_criterion: apps/theorem-federation/Cargo.toml pins a compatible iroh family; cargo check/test for that crate resolves the dependency graph; implementation notes name any API mismatch around external outboards or relay proof.
items.verification: Pinned iroh 1.0.1, iroh-blobs 0.103.0, and iroh-gossip 0.101.0 in apps/theorem-federation/Cargo.toml. cargo info showed all require Rust 1.91; local rustc is 1.95.0. CARGO_TARGET_DIR='/Volumes/SSD Samsung/cargo-targets/theorem-federation' cargo test --manifest-path apps/theorem-federation/Cargo.toml passed 10 tests, including live federation_network integration tests; cargo clippy --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings passed.

items.deliverable: D1. Peer identity as the iroh endpoint.
items.acceptance_criterion: A node started twice on the same data dir presents the same EndpointId. Two configured peers have a dialable identity surface for authenticated QUIC under the federation ALPN.
items.verification: identity_is_stable_for_data_dir passes. authenticated_quic_stream_applies_structured_and_text_delta binds two loopback Iroh endpoints under ALPN theorem/federation/1, asserts the remote EndpointId on both sides, opens an authenticated QUIC stream, and exchanges federation frames. theorem-federation doctor --bind-endpoint with THEOREM_FEDERATION_ENABLED=1 bound an N0 endpoint and reported relay mode n0_public_relays reachable.
items.deferral_reason: Two physical nodes on different networks were not run in this session, so cross-network hole-punch versus relay-routed behavior remains not-run.

items.deliverable: D2. The blob lane, convergence and bootstrap.
items.acceptance_criterion: A sha256-addressed pack/object/document can be mapped to a Blake3 transfer link without rewriting sha256 identity, verified on receipt, and represented as the source for the existing graph-version checkout/bootstrap path.
items.verification: BlobResolver records sha256:<hex> identity and blake3:<hex> transfer links for canonical bytes, resolves both directions, and verify_transfer_boundary rejects changed bytes. iroh_blobs_transfers_pack_bytes_and_records_dual_hash_boundary starts a real iroh-blobs provider over a loopback Iroh Router, downloads by BlobTicket into a second MemStore, reads bytes back with get_bytes, and records the sha256/BLAKE3 boundary through the trust-gated peer accept path.
items.deferral_reason: Interrupted/resumable transfer recovery, cold-node graph-version checkout from a peer, and externally owned outboard over existing sha256 files were not run.

items.acceptance_criterion: Federation frames carry SubstratePeer structured StampedBatch deltas and yrs update_v1 bytes without changing SubstratePeer merge functions; frame encode/decode is tested and bounded.
items.verification: FederationFrame carries StampedBatch, text update_v1/state-vector bytes, and PeerEvent presence. delta_frame_round_trips_over_length_prefixed_stream passed over a tokio duplex stream with an 8 MiB frame cap. authenticated_quic_stream_applies_structured_and_text_delta moves structured and text deltas over a real Iroh QUIC stream, applies them through apply_inbound_frame into SubstratePeer, sends a concurrent server-side structured delta back over the same stream, and asserts the local exchange completes under one second with both edits retained.
items.deferral_reason: The full version-log assertion against graph-version checkout state was not run; the local SubstratePeer convergence oracle verifies retained merged object properties.

items.acceptance_criterion: Presence events are encoded for a deterministic iroh-gossip TopicId derived from tenant/scope, so three peers can join the same awareness topic without pairwise fanout.
items.verification: awareness_topic_id deterministically derives an iroh_gossip::TopicId from tenant/scope, and AwarenessMessage encodes/decodes PeerEvent::Presence. three_peer_gossip_mesh_delivers_awareness_payload binds three loopback Iroh endpoints, routes iroh-gossip through Routers, bootstraps the mesh with MemoryLookup, broadcasts an encoded PeerEvent::Presence, and verifies both other peers receive and decode it.

items.deliverable: D5. Trust as policy over the transport.
items.acceptance_criterion: A per-EndpointId EMA trust table rejects below-floor inbound blob/delta contributions before merge and admits above-floor contributions without modifying CRDT merge code.
items.verification: TrustPolicy records EMA outcomes by EndpointId, rejects below-floor peers via require_inbound, and admits the same peer when the floor is adjusted. apply_inbound_frame calls require_inbound before merge/text apply, BlobResolver::record_bytes_from_peer calls require_inbound before recording received bytes, authenticated_quic_stream_applies_structured_and_text_delta proves above-floor reconciliation, and trust_rejection_prevents_delta_and_blob_acceptance proves below-floor delta/blob rejection leaves merge state and resolver mappings unchanged.

items.deliverable: D6. The federation driver and the relay.
items.acceptance_criterion: A default-off driver reads federation config, reports endpoint/relay/peer status through theorem-federation and scripts/theorem, and can bind the endpoint when explicitly enabled.
items.verification: theorem-federation status is default-off and reports identity/relay/peer warnings. scripts/theorem federation status succeeded with a temp data dir. THEOREM_FEDERATION_ENABLED=1 theorem-federation doctor --bind-endpoint bound an endpoint and reported reachable relay mode.
items.deferral_reason: Configured peer dialing, connected vs relay-routed peer status, and forced relay fallback with no data loss were not run.

items.acceptance_criterion: CLAUDE.md and docs/site/reference/apps.md document apps/theorem-federation; scripts/theorem exposes federation commands without disrupting the existing dirty graph/Yazi command; doc drift is refreshed.
items.verification: Updated CLAUDE.md, docs/site/reference/apps.md, scripts/theorem, and .harness/doc-map-baseline.apps. bash -n scripts/theorem, git diff --check for this slice, ./scripts/check-doc-drift.sh --refresh, and ./scripts/check-doc-drift.sh --full all passed.

execution_receipt.note: Extended the standalone Iroh federation lane from scaffold to local live proofs: authenticated QUIC frame exchange, bidirectional SubstratePeer convergence, real iroh-blobs provider/get transfer, three-peer gossip awareness, and trust-gated blob/delta acceptance. Remaining gaps require multi-node NAT/relay, interrupted resume, and graph-version bootstrap labs.
